pattern: Precomputing, for every pattern character, the set of source characters it may become reduces the search to a sliding window of allowed matches.
hints: A pattern character matches a window character if they are already equal or the mapping permits turning the former into the latter.
Collect for each pattern character the set of characters it can legally become (including itself), then test every aligned window of the source.

Build a map from each character to its allowed replacements plus itself. Slide a window of the pattern's length over the source; a window matches if at every offset the source character is in the corresponding pattern character's allowed set.

edge_inputs.description: no mapping allows zero to become o
empty mappings still matches an exact substring
empty mappings with no exact substring fails
whole string equals pattern after one mapping
mapping direction matters and must not be reversed

stress.description: random source and pattern over a small alphabet with random mappings
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abcdef012'
    sn = max(1, min(size, 300))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(sn))
    subn = rng.randrange(1, sn + 1)
    sub = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(subn))
    mlen = rng.randrange(0, 12)
    mappings = []
    for _ in range(mlen):
        a = rng.choice(alpha)
        b = rng.choice(alpha)
        if a != b:
            mappings.append([a, b])
    return [s, sub, mappings]
